name: IRUSA Waqf
mission: ESTABLISH A POOL OF CAPITAL THAT ENSURES THE LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF IRUSA AND ENABLES THE CHARITY IN ACHIEVING ITS OBJECTIVES.
theoryOfChange: Zakat described as purification/growth/blessing and distributed according to Islamic principles; Sadaqah Jariyah described as continuous charity that supports long-term projects (water systems, clinics, schools, farms, small businesses) that benefit communities for generations.
scoreSummary: Irusa Waqf shows good alignment and moderate impact (minor risk deductions applied).

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 30/50, the charity offers a high donor fit for those seeking to contribute sadaqah toward sustainable, religiously-aligned institutional growth [3][4]. The mission focuses on the urgency of cultural and religious preservation through permanent charitable capital [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization maintains a program expense ratio of 80.1%, directing the majority of its $2,405,280 in total revenue toward its endowment and distribution goals [1][2]. While it operates with a direct service model, the impact score of 26/50 reflects a balanced assessment of its cost per beneficiary and the long-term nature of endowment growth [2].

The organization manages the WAQF IRUSA Endowment and various distribution programs to provide lasting financial stability for charitable initiatives [1][3].

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="9">392.9 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring it can sustain operations for over 30 years without new funding.
The charity operates with a <cite id="12">fundraising efficiency of $0.06 per $1 raised</cite>, keeping donor acquisition costs remarkably low.
Despite its endowment structure, it maintains an <cite id="13">80.1% program expense ratio</cite> to support its core mission.

strengths_deep_dive: Irusa Waqf is distinguished by its institutional maturity, having operated for <cite id="2">over 30 years</cite> since its founding in 1992. Its financial model is built for endurance, evidenced by <cite id="8">net assets exceeding $15 million</cite> and a <cite id="14">100% independent board</cite> providing oversight. This structure allows for consistent support of long-term projects, such as <cite id="15">sponsoring 29,204 orphans</cite> as reported in recent filings. By focusing on the <cite id="3">establishment of capital pools</cite>, the organization creates a reliable safety net for international relief efforts.
rich_narrative.summary: Irusa Waqf operates as a strategic endowment fund designed to provide <cite id="1">long-term sustainability for Islamic Relief USA</cite>. Established in <cite id="2">1992</cite>, the organization focuses on building a <cite id="3">permanent pool of capital</cite> to ensure that humanitarian missions can continue even during periods of economic volatility. Unlike traditional relief organizations that spend all incoming donations immediately, Irusa Waqf manages the <cite id="4">WAQF IRUSA Endowment</cite> alongside programs for <cite id="5">Sadaqah Jariyah and orphan sponsorship</cite>. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="6">total revenue of $1,912,900</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="7">$753,608 in public contributions</cite>. The fund maintains a significant asset base, holding <cite id="8">$15,291,521 in total assets</cite> to support its long-term objectives. This model prioritizes institutional longevity, allowing the charity to maintain <cite id="9">392.9 months of working capital</cite>, which far exceeds typical humanitarian benchmarks. While the endowment structure limits immediate liquidity for rapid-response aid, it provides a <cite id="10">documented theory of change</cite> focused on perpetual support for vulnerable populations across <cite id="11">Gaza, Yemen, and the United States</cite>.
